# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_dns_resolver (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Specifies the name of the DNS resolver.

### Optional

- `answer_default_zones` (Boolean) Specifies whether the system answers DNS queries for the default zones localhost, reverse 127.0.0.1 and ::1, and AS112. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is `false`, meaning the system passes along the DNS queries for the default zones.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `cache_size` (Number) Specifies the size of the internal DNS resolver cache. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is 5767168 bytes. After the cache reaches this size, when new or refreshed content arrives, the system removes expired and older content and caches the new or updated content.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `randomize_query_case` (Boolean) When `true`, specifies the internal DNS resolver randomizes character case in domain name queries issued to the root DNS servers. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is `true`.
- `route_domain` (Number) Specifies the route domain the resolver uses for outbound traffic.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the resource exists. When `absent`, ensures the resource is removed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_ipv4` (Boolean) Specifies whether the system can use IPv4 to query backend nameservers. An IPv4 Self IP and default route must be available for these queries to work successfully. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is `true`.
- `use_ipv6` (Boolean) Specifies whether the system can use IPv6 to query backend nameservers. An IPv6 Self IP and default route must be available for these queries to work successfully. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is `true`.
- `use_tcp` (Boolean) Specifies whether the system answers and issues TCP-formatted queries. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is `true`.
- `use_udp` (Boolean) Specifies whether the system answers and issues UDP-formatted queries. When creating a new resolver, if this parameter is not specified, the default is `true`.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
